sections.heading: Required Roles
sections.body:
To [insert](atlas-ui-insert-documents), [edit](atlas-ui-edit-document),
or [delete documents](atlas-ui-delete-document), you must have been
granted access through one of the following roles:

- [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) or [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>)

- [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>)

- [Project Data Access Read/Write](<Project Data Access Read/Write>)

sections.heading: Insert Documents
sections.body:
To add one or more documents to a collection through the
Atlas UI, you can specify the documents to insert
from scratch or you can clone an existing document and modify its
fields and values as needed.

To insert documents into your collection, use one of the following methods in
Atlas:

- **JSON Mode :icon-lg:\`CurlyBraces\`**: Allows you to write or paste JSON
  documents in the editor. Use this mode to insert multiple documents at once
  as an array.

- **Field-by-Field Editor :icon-lg:\`Menu\`**: Allows you to select individual
  field values and types for each inserted document. You can only insert one
  document at a time using this method.

JSON Mode

In the drop-down menu, select Insert Document.

The document editor appears with the `_id` field, which contains
an [ObjectId](manual/reference/bson-types) value that reflects the time when the document
was created, not when it was inserted. As such, the `ObjectId`
does not represent a strict insertion order.

Type or paste the documents you want to insert into your
collection. To insert multiple documents, enter a comma-separated
array of JSON documents.

For example, the following array inserts 5 documents into
the collection:

```json
[
   { "_id" : 8752, "title" : "Divine Comedy", "author" : "Dante", "copies" : 1 },
   { "_id" : 7000, "title" : "The Odyssey", "author" : "Homer", "copies" : 10 },
   { "_id" : 7020, "title" : "Iliad", "author" : "Homer", "copies" : 10 },
   { "_id" : 8645, "title" : "Eclogues", "author" : "Dante", "copies" : 2 },
   { "_id" : 8751, "title" : "The Banquet", "author" : "Dante", "copies" : 2 }
]
```

Field-by-Field Editor

In the drop-down menu, select Insert Document.

The document editor appears with the `_id` field, which contains
an [ObjectId](manual/reference/bson-types) value that reflects the time when the document
was created, not when it was inserted. As such, the `ObjectId`
does not represent a strict insertion order.

In the document editor, click the  icon in the
View toggle.

To add a new field, hover over the field row number of an existing
field and click the  icon.

For each field in the document, fill in the field name, set a field
value, and select a field type.

> **Note:** To change the `_id` field to a custom value, change the field
> type from `ObjectId` to `string`. You can then overwrite the
> `_id` value.

sections.heading: Clone Documents
sections.body:
You can insert new documents by cloning the schema and values of
an existing document in a collection.

List View

To clone a document, hover over the desired document
and click the Clone button.

![Clone button](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/clone-doc.png)

JSON View

To clone a document, hover over the desired document
and click the Clone button.

![Clone button JSON](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/json-view-clone.png)

Table View

To clone a document, hover over the desired document
and click the Clone button.

![Clone button Table view](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/table-view-clone.png)

When you click the Clone button, Atlas opens the
document insertion dialog with the same schema and values as the cloned
document. You can edit any of these fields and values before you insert
the new document.

sections.heading: View Documents
sections.body:
To view and manage documents, you can use one of the following views in the
**Documents** tab:

- **List View :icon-lg:\`Menu\`**: The default document view. Atlas shows
  documents in a list. In this view, you can expand embedded objects and arrays.

- **JSON View :icon-lg:\`CurlyBraces\`**: Atlas shows documents as
  properly-formatted JSON objects. Atlas uses extended JSON to display the
  data types of each field.

- **Table View :icon-lg:\`Table\`**: Atlas shows documents as rows of a table,
  with document fields shown in the table columns. With this view, you can see
  which documents contain specific field values.

sections.heading: Expand Embedded Objects and Arrays
sections.body:
**List-view:**

List View

To expand embedded objects and array elements, hover over the document
and click the arrow in the top-left corner.

![Expand embedded objects list](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/expand-doc-list-view.png)

**Json:**

JSON View

To expand embedded objects and array elements, hover over the
document and click the top arrow on the left side of the document.

To expand individual objects and arrays, click the arrow to
the left of the desired field.

![Expand embedded objects JSON](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/expand-doc-json-view.png)

**Table-view:**

Table View

To view nested object fields and array elements, hover your cursor
over a field with a value type of `Object` or `Array` and
click the button with outward-pointing arrows which appears on the
right side of the field.

Atlas opens a new tab in the Table View corresponding
to the nested element.

![View nested elements in Table view](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/table-view-expand-nested-elem.png)

The following example displays the `address`
object nested within the `restaurants` collection documents. The
new tab displays the five properties of the `address` object:

![Nested documents](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/doc-table-view-nested.png)

To return to the original table display, click the first tab
in the Table View which displays the name of your collection.

sections.heading: Copy Documents to Clipboard
sections.body:
**List-view:**

List View

To copy a document to your clipboard, hover over the document
and click the copy **Copy** icon:

![Copy Document from List View](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/copy-doc-list-view.png)

**Json:**

JSON View

To copy a document to your clipboard, hover over the document
and click the copy **Copy** icon:

![Copy Document from JSON View](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/copy-doc-json-view.png)

**Table-view:**

Table View

To copy a document to your clipboard, hover over the document
and click the copy **Copy** icon:

![Copy Document from Table View](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/copy-doc-table-view.png)

sections.heading: Number of Documents Displayed per Page
sections.body:
The Atlas UI limits the *total byte size* of documents shown per
page. As a result, you may see varying numbers of documents per page,
especially if your documents vary significantly in size.

sections.heading: Edit One Document
sections.body:
When you edit a document in List or Table view, Atlas performs a
[findOneAndUpdate](manual/reference/method/db.collection.findOneAndUpdate)
operation and updates only those fields that you have
changed.

When you edit a document in JSON view, Atlas performs a
[findOneAndReplace](manual/reference/method/db.collection.findOneAndReplace)
operation and replaces the document.

List View

To modify a document, hover over the document and click the pencil
icon:

![Document Edit Selection](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/edit-doc.png)

After you click the pencil icon, the document enters edit mode.
You can now make changes to the fields, values, or data types
of values.

To delete a field from a document, click the  icon to
the left of the field:

![Document Deletion](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/edit-doc3.png)

Once selected, the field is marked for removal and appears
highlighted in red. Atlas asks for confirmation that you want to
update the document by removing the field.

To add a new field in the document after an existing field, hover
over the row number in the dialog and click on the plus sign. The
row number is not part of the document but is part of the dialog
display.

![Add a field to a document](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/add-field.png)

You can also add a new field at the end of the document by
pressing the tab key when your text cursor is in the value of the
last document field.

To modify documents, click on existing field names or
values and make changes. In this example, the `borough` was
changed from `Manhattan` to `Queens`. Changed fields appear highlighted in
yellow:

![Document Update View](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/update-field.png)

When you edit a document in List or Table view, Atlas performs a
[findOneAndUpdate](manual/reference/method/db.collection.findOneAndUpdate)
operation and updates only those fields that you have
changed.

If Atlas detects that you have changed fields
that were modified outside of Atlas, it notifies you, preventing
you from accidentally overwriting the changes made outside of Atlas.
You can choose to proceed and replace the document by clicking
Replace, or cancel your changes.

When you are finished editing the document, click the `Update`
button to commit your changes.

To revert changes to a document, hover over the edited field
and click the revert icon which appears to the left
of the field's line number.

![Revert Document in List View](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/revert-doc-list-view.png)

JSON View

To modify a document, hover over the document and click the pencil
icon:

![Document Edit Selection in JSON View](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/document-edit-json.png)

After you click the pencil icon, the document enters edit mode.
You can now add, remove, and edit field values by modifying
the JSON document.

By default, this view hides embedded objects and arrays. To expand
embedded objects and array elements, hover over the target
document and click the top arrow on the left side of the document.

To expand individual objects and arrays, click the arrow to
the left of the desired field.

![Expand embedded objects in JSON view](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/expand-doc-json-view.png)

When you edit a document in JSON view, Atlas performs a
[findOneAndReplace](manual/reference/method/db.collection.findOneAndReplace)
operation and replaces the document.

If Atlas detects that you have changed fields
that were modified outside of Atlas, it notifies you, preventing
you from accidentally overwriting the changes made outside of Atlas.
You can choose to proceed and replace the document by clicking
Replace, or cancel your changes.

Table View

To modify a document, hover over the document and click the pencil
icon:

![Document Edit Selection in Table View](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/table-view-modify.png)

After you click the pencil icon, the document enters edit mode.

When you edit a document in List or Table view, Atlas performs a
[findOneAndUpdate](manual/reference/method/db.collection.findOneAndUpdate)
operation and updates only those fields that you have
changed.

If Atlas detects that you have changed fields
that were modified outside of Atlas, it notifies you, preventing
you from accidentally overwriting the changes made outside of Atlas.
You can choose to proceed and replace the document by clicking
Replace, or cancel your changes.

To delete a field from a document:

- Click the value of the field you want to delete.

- Click the  icon.

- Click Update to confirm your changes.

To add a new field to the document:

- Click the field after which you wish to add the new field.

- Click the  icon.

- Click Add Field after \<Field Name>.

- Populate your newly created field.

- Click Update to confirm your changes.

While modifying a document, you have the option to revert changes
made to a field prior to saving the modified document.

Click the revert icon which appears on the
right side of the edited table element.

![Document Revert Changes in Table View](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/revert-doc-table-view.png)

sections.heading: Edit Multiple Documents
sections.body:
You can use the bulk update operations workflow to update multiple
documents in Atlas. For details, see [Modify Multiple Documents](atlas-ui-bulk-update).

sections.heading: Delete One Document
sections.body:
To delete a document from a collection through the Atlas UI:

List View

To delete a document, hover over the document and click the
 icon.

![Delete button in List view](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/delete-doc.png)

After you click the delete button, the document is flagged for deletion.
Atlas asks for confirmation that you want to remove the
document.

JSON View

To delete a document, hover over the document and click the
 icon.

![Delete button in JSON view](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/delete-doc-json.png)

After you click the delete button, the document is flagged for deletion.
Atlas asks for confirmation that you want to remove the
document.

Table View

To delete a document, hover over the document and click the
 icon.

![Delete button Table view](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/table-view-delete.png)

After you click the delete button, the document is flagged for deletion.
Atlas asks for confirmation that you want to remove the
document.

Once you confirm, Atlas deletes the document from the collection.

sections.heading: Learn More
sections.body:
- [Query Your Data](atlas-ui-query-bar-filter)

- [Modify Multiple Documents](atlas-ui-bulk-update)

- [Delete Multiple Documents](atlas-ui-bulk-delete).
